description: Build the hookli docs + landing site (Next.js App Router, Tailwind, dark-first brand) to premium quality: catchy landing, a doc page with live interactive demo for every hook, search, donation placeholder, OG images, full polish. Benchmark: usehooks-ts.com. One task per iteration; ralph/check.sh (lint + tsc + next build) must be green before every commit.

tasks.title: Research benchmark docs sites and write docs/DESIGN.md
tasks.notes: Study usehooks-ts.com (landing + a hook page like /react-hook/use-boolean), react-use, and mantine.dev/hooks via WebFetch/WebSearch. Capture: information architecture, landing section order, hook-page anatomy (description/demo/code/API), sidebar+search patterns, what makes them feel premium.
acceptance: docs/DESIGN.md exists with: IA sitemap, landing section list, hook-page template spec, component inventory (Header, Sidebar, HookDemo, CodeBlock, CopyButton, Footer...), and hookli brand tokens (#0B1120 / #61DAFB / #475569 / #94A3B8, monospace headings)
Decisions are concrete (no 'maybe/or'), so later tasks can build without re-deciding
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Design system + app shell
tasks.notes: Implement DESIGN.md tokens as CSS variables + Tailwind theme. Replace create-next-app boilerplate entirely.
acceptance: globals.css defines brand tokens (ground #0B1120, accent #61DAFB, slate #475569, gray #94A3B8) as CSS vars wired into Tailwind
Root layout: metadata (title template 'hookli — %s'), favicon from /hookli-icon.svg, monospace heading font + sans body via next/font
Header (use(hookli) wordmark linking home, Docs link, GitHub link) and Footer (npm, GitHub, license) shared components
Default boilerplate page/svgs removed
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Landing hero — catchy, code-first
tasks.notes: The hero IS the pitch. use(hookli) wordmark treatment, tagline, copyable `npm i hookli` command, CTA to /docs.
acceptance: Hero: big monospace use(hookli) headline with cyan accent + blinking cursor (CSS animation, respects prefers-reduced-motion)
Tagline: 'Simple React hooks. Typed. SSR-safe. Zero dependencies.'
Install command box with working copy-to-clipboard button (visual feedback on copy)
Primary CTA 'Explore the docs' -> /docs; secondary link to GitHub
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Landing features + live code preview section
tasks.notes: Why-hookli grid + a real code sample rendered with syntax highlighting next to a LIVE working demo (e.g. useToggle) to prove it.
acceptance: Feature grid (6 items: zero deps, TypeScript-first, SSR-safe, tree-shakable, ESM+CJS, 11 hooks) with inline SVG icons — no emoji
Split section: highlighted code sample on one side, the same hook running live on the other
Responsive at 375/768/1024/1440
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Docs infrastructure: registry, /docs layout, sidebar
tasks.notes: Central hook registry drives everything (sidebar, pages, search, landing counts). One source of truth.
acceptance: lib/hooks-registry.ts: all 11 hooks with slug, name, one-line description, category (state/effects/dom/data), signature summary
/docs layout with sidebar (grouped by category, active state) + /docs index page introducing the library
Dynamic route /docs/[slug] scaffolded, generateStaticParams from the registry, notFound for unknown slugs
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: HookDemo + CodeBlock framework
tasks.notes: Reusable machinery every hook page uses: live preview pane + code with copy button. This is the site's core differentiator.
acceptance: components/HookDemo: client component wrapper with Preview/Code tabs, consistent frame, error boundary so a demo crash never kills the page
components/CodeBlock: syntax-highlighted TS/TSX (shiki or lightweight equivalent) + CopyButton with feedback
Hook-page template composes: title, description, live demo, usage code, API table (parameters/returns), 'view source on GitHub' link
Demonstrated end-to-end on ONE hook page (useToggle) as the reference implementation
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Hook pages batch 1 — state: useDebounce, useForm, useLocalStorage
tasks.notes: Follow the T6 reference page exactly. Each demo must be genuinely interactive (type-to-debounce, live form values, persisted input).
acceptance: Three complete pages with live demos importing from 'hookli', usage code, API tables
Demos handle SSR prerender safely (next build green)
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Hook pages batch 2 — storage/theme: useLocalStorageWithExpiry, useDarkMode, useToggle-polish
tasks.notes: useLocalStorageWithExpiry demo shows TTL countdown + expiry. useDarkMode demo toggles a scoped preview panel (not the whole site theme). Revisit the T6 useToggle page to match any template refinements.
acceptance: useLocalStorageWithExpiry + useDarkMode pages complete with interactive demos and API tables
useToggle reference page consistent with the final template
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Hook pages batch 3 — DOM: useClickOutside, useMousePosition, useInfiniteScroll
tasks.notes: useClickOutside: dropdown demo. useMousePosition: tracked coordinates inside a bounded panel. useInfiniteScroll: scrollable list demo loading mock items (scoped container).
acceptance: Three complete pages with genuinely interactive demos, usage code, API tables
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Hook pages batch 4 — data/device: useFetch, useGeoLocation
tasks.notes: useFetch: fetch from a public JSON API (jsonplaceholder) with loading/error states visible. useGeoLocation: permission-aware demo with graceful denied state (never auto-request on page load).
acceptance: Both pages complete; all 11 registry hooks now have live doc pages
Geolocation demo requests permission only on user click
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Search — command palette over the registry
tasks.notes: Client-side ⌘K / Ctrl+K palette filtering the hook registry by name/description/category. No external search service.
acceptance: Keyboard-openable (⌘K) + header search button; arrow-key navigation; Enter routes to the hook page; Esc closes
Accessible: focus trap, aria roles, works at 375px
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Support/donate placeholder + contributing section
tasks.notes: Tasteful 'Support hookli' presence WITHOUT real payment links (placeholder hrefs '#' + TODO comments for GitHub Sponsors/Ko-fi later).
acceptance: Landing section + /support page: sponsor card, 'star on GitHub' (real link), contribute card linking CONTRIBUTING notes
All payment links are clearly placeholder ('#'), no fabricated accounts
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: SEO, OG images, metadata polish
tasks.notes: Per-page metadata from the registry; OG image via opengraph-image (brand banner style: solid #0B1120, use(hookli) mark); sitemap + robots.
acceptance: Root + per-hook metadata (title, description, openGraph); opengraph-image renders the brand lockup
app/sitemap.ts and app/robots.ts generated from the registry
favicon/apple-icon wired from the {h} icon
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Polish pass — responsive, a11y, motion, 404
tasks.notes: Sweep every page against the quality bar in prompt.md.
acceptance: No horizontal scroll at 375px anywhere; sidebar collapses to a mobile drawer/menu
Visible focus states everywhere; body contrast AA; all micro-interactions 150-300ms; prefers-reduced-motion disables cursor blink + transitions
Custom branded not-found page
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Brand v3 recolor — new assets, token system (colors + fonts)
tasks.notes: The brand changed (2026-07-12): assets in public/ are ALREADY the new ones (hookli-banner.svg/.png, hookli-icon.svg — Prussian tile, teal hook cradling a white state dot, Avenir wordmark with teal full stop). The site still uses the OLD tokens from T2 (#0B1120 ground, #61DAFB accent, monospace headings) — replace them everywhere via the token layer, not per-component edits.
acceptance: globals.css/Tailwind tokens swapped to brand v3: ground #003748 (Prussian Blue), accent #30C5CA (Scooter), ink #FFFFFF, secondary/muted #8FB6C2 (mist) — derive hover/surface/border shades from these (solid colors, no gradients); every page picks the change up through tokens only

Typography: replace monospace-heading treatment with a premium geometric sans via next/font (DM Sans or Plus Jakarta Sans — the Avenir-class Google option); body stays a clean sans; monospace remains ONLY for code blocks

Header wordmark: hookli. (lowercase + teal full stop) matching the new banner — the use(hookli) monospace lockup is retired; hero updated to the same treatment with the new mark (inline SVG from the icon)
Favicon/apple-icon wired from public/hookli-icon.svg; landing + OG surfaces use the new banner assets
Contrast verified: white on #003748 and #30C5CA usage meet WCAG AA for their text sizes
No stray old-brand hexes remain (grep 0B1120/61DAFB/475569 clean, except code-syntax theme)
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: usehooks-ts anatomy parity — ONE reusable hook-page template
tasks.notes: Benchmark screenshots studied 2026-07-12: usehooks-ts hook pages follow a strict template (Title -> one-liner with linked Web-API chip -> Usage code with line numbers + highlighted call line -> API signature with linked types -> Parameters table with Default chips -> Returns -> Type-aliases tables -> Hook = embedded implementation source) inside a 3-column layout with a right-rail On-This-Page TOC. Refactor toward that, keeping EVERYTHING data-driven: one HookPage template component rendered from the registry + HOOK_DOCS — zero per-page bespoke layouts. Adding a new hook must require ONLY a registry/HOOK_DOCS entry + one demo component.
acceptance: Single reusable HookPage template renders all 11 pages from registry + HOOK_DOCS data; no page-specific layout code remains under app/docs/[slug]
Right-rail On This Page TOC (Demo / Usage / API / Hook sections, active-section highlight, hidden on mobile) present on every hook page
CodeBlock supports line numbers + an optional highlighted line (the hook-call line) used by Usage snippets

Each page has a Hook section embedding the actual hookli implementation source for that hook (vendored snapshot checked into lib/hook-sources.ts, accurate to the installed hookli version) with a view-on-GitHub link
Parameters tables show Default-value chips; type aliases render as their own Name/Type/Description tables; Web-API mentions in descriptions are linked chips (MDN)
Right rail reserves a support slot below the TOC (placeholder consistent with T12)
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Engineering conventions — port the Nzmly CLAUDE.md style, align the codebase
tasks.notes: READ-ONLY reference: /Users/s-m/Developer/platform/CLAUDE.md plus any frontend/*/CLAUDE.md there (reading is allowed; never modify that repo or ../rehooks-app). Saif wants the STYLE of that conventions doc — not its Nzmly content: file-name casing rules, code structure, folder structure, module organization, component conventions. Author the equivalent for THIS app, then make the codebase actually comply.
acceptance: AGENTS.md (referenced by CLAUDE.md) gains a Conventions section in the Nzmly doc style: explicit rules with examples for file-name case, folder structure (modules/feature grouping), component file anatomy (props type, export style, client/server placement), hooks/lib/registry organization, import order, and styling-token usage
Rules are DECISIVE (one convention per topic, no maybes) and each rule shows a right/wrong example
Codebase aligned to the written rules: files renamed to the chosen casing, folders regrouped per the module structure, imports updated — repo passes its own conventions
ralph/prompt.md quality bar references the conventions so later iterations follow them
bash ralph/check.sh is green

tasks.title: Final QA + site README
tasks.notes: Walk the full sitemap as a checklist; fix anything below premium; document the app.
acceptance: Checklist in progress.txt: every route (/, /docs, 11 hook pages, /support, 404) verified against DESIGN.md and prompt.md quality bar, with fixes applied
README.md rewritten for this app: what it is, stack, dev/build commands, structure, how to add a new hook page (registry + demo)
bash ralph/check.sh is green
Don't push or deploy — leave that to the human
